P3CON-001.title: AI at Work 2025: Momentum Builds, but Gaps Remain
P3CON-001.author: BCG
P3CON-001.excerpt: BCG hired 1,000 additional staff in 2024 specifically for AI services; BCG transformed from zero to 20% AI revenue ($2.7 billion) in just two years. AI-using consultants completed tasks 25.1% faster with over 40% higher quality in the Harvard/BCG study; junior analysts gained 30-40% efficiency, experienced staff 20-30%.
P3CON-001.relevance: Quantifies AI revenue contribution and productivity gains at a flagship strategy firm
P3CON-001.credibility: high - primary publisher BCG, widely cited Harvard study referenced

P3CON-002.title: Rewiring the way McKinsey works with Lilli
P3CON-002.author: McKinsey & Company
P3CON-002.excerpt: Lilli is McKinsey's proprietary GenAI platform with RAG over 40 curated knowledge sources, 100,000+ documents, network of experts in 70 countries. >75% of 43,000 employees use it monthly; 500,000+ monthly prompts; ~17 weekly launches per user. One-Click PowerPoint agent stitches templates, charts, narrative into client-ready decks.
P3CON-002.relevance: Direct evidence of deep AI penetration into core consultant workflow at the prestige leader
P3CON-002.credibility: high - primary source from McKinsey itself

P3CON-003.title: 10 Ways McKinsey Is Using AI - Case Studies
P3CON-003.author: DigitalDefynd
P3CON-003.excerpt: Internal time-and-motion studies show each Lilli session eliminates ~6 minutes of manual document hunting; across 500,000 monthly prompts that's 50,000+ consultant hours (~$12M in fully-loaded labor) redeployed to higher-value analysis. 30% productivity increase reported for certain tasks. McKinsey's model now involves 20,000 AI agents alongside 40,000 humans.
P3CON-003.relevance: Translates AI adoption into concrete labor-hours and dollar productivity figures
P3CON-003.credibility: medium - third-party blog but references McKinsey internal numbers consistently reported elsewhere

P3CON-004.title: The AI Revolution in Big 4 Consulting Firms
P3CON-004.author: Big4Events
P3CON-004.excerpt: Deloitte (PairD), Bain (Sage), McKinsey (Lilli), EY (EYQ), PwC (ChatPwC), Accenture, KPMG (Workbench), BCG all deployed internal GPT-4-class assistants to tens of thousands of consultants on essentially the same 2023-2024 timeline. EY committed $1.4B already-invested; PwC $1B over 3 years US; KPMG multibillion over 5 years on Microsoft cloud/AI.
P3CON-004.relevance: Establishes that internal AI assistant deployment is universal across MBB and Big 4
P3CON-004.credibility: medium-high - aggregates publicly known firm announcements

P3CON-005.title: AI Micro-Consulting: Premium Rates, Solo Practice
P3CON-005.author: Digital Applied
P3CON-005.excerpt: Solo experts charge $200-$500/hour by pairing domain knowledge with AI. What used to require an agency and $8,000 can be delivered by a solo consultant in 48 hours. A 48-hour competitive analysis that took four weeks manually now commands $1,500-$3,000. Five niches: financial modeling, marketing audits, ops optimization, compliance review, competitive analysis.
P3CON-005.relevance: Quantifies how AI changes the unit economics of solo/indie consulting - the segment most relevant to our product
P3CON-005.credibility: medium - blog from a service vendor, but numbers consistent with cross-reference

P3CON-006.title: The Rise of the Portfolio Career: Why 2025 Is the Year of Fractional Work
P3CON-006.author: Fractionus
P3CON-006.excerpt: LinkedIn profiles mentioning fractional roles grew from 2,000 in 2022 to 110,000 in early 2024. Fractional marketing leaders grew from ~60,000 to 120,000 between 2022-2024. Demand for fractional leaders grew 68% YoY; Toptal 2024 survey shows ~46% YoY demand growth. 25% of US businesses have adopted fractional hiring, projected 35% by 2025. Fractional CFOs and CMOs lead - finance ~25%, marketing ~20%, COOs ~15%, CTOs ~10%. Global fractional market $5.7B 2024 -> $19.1B 2033 (14.2% CAGR).
P3CON-006.relevance: Hard numbers on the fractional executive boom - this is the most product-relevant subsegment of consulting
P3CON-006.credibility: medium-high - aggregates Toptal, EY, Frak Conference data; numbers consistent across sources

P3CON-007.title: Top 2025 Trends for Fractional Executives to Succeed as Leadership Solopreneurs
P3CON-007.author: Fractional Officer
P3CON-007.excerpt: There are over 100,000 fractional executives and consultants in the market. By 2025, clients expect fractionals to leverage AI-driven technology and data insights. Fractional executives should update their online presence emphasizing niche; the more specific, the easier ideal clients can find them. Fractional execs typically cost 1/3 to 1/2 of a full-time CXO.
P3CON-007.relevance: Confirms fractional segment size and personal-brand pressure

P3CON-008.title: How AI Is Upending How Consulting Firms Hire Talent
P3CON-008.author: Harvard Business Review
P3CON-008.excerpt: Traditional consulting model: hire large cohorts of junior associates for heavy lifting with very low partner-track conversion (1-2 per 100 at prestige firms) relying on attrition. AI is dismantling this hiring model; entry-level roles shrinking; firms shifting from hiring for grunt work to hiring for leadership potential.
P3CON-008.relevance: Foundational HBR claim that AI is structurally changing the consulting talent pyramid
P3CON-008.credibility: high - HBR primary source (accessed via excerpt; full article paywalled)

P3CON-009.title: McKinsey Plans 10% Layoffs Affecting Thousands Because of AI
P3CON-009.author: FinalRoundAI
P3CON-009.excerpt: McKinsey cut ~200 technology and support staff late 2025; GMP Bob Sternfels signaled additional non-client role reductions over next two years. Bain, Deloitte, KPMG, Accenture also laid off workers in 2025; PwC and EY pushed pay cuts and start-date deferrals. AI is replacing junior consultants in their core function of synthesis. By 2025, AI roles outnumbered entry-level consultant positions.
P3CON-009.relevance: Evidence that AI is not just augmenting but reducing junior consultant headcount across major firms
P3CON-009.credibility: medium - aggregator referencing primary firm announcements

P3CON-010.title: McKinsey AI Interview Being Piloted As A Part of Final Round Interviews
P3CON-010.author: Management Consulted
P3CON-010.excerpt: McKinsey introduced live, time-bound exercise where candidates use Lilli to work through a consulting business problem - prompting AI, reviewing output, refining questions, synthesizing into recommendations - with interviewer observing collaboration. Piloted late 2025 for select US final rounds, primarily Business Analyst candidates. AI literacy now baseline expectation across all MBB. AI cases in ~1 in 3 first-round interviews at MBB.
P3CON-010.relevance: Shows AI capability has moved from preferred to required at hiring gate of the most prestigious firm
P3CON-010.credibility: high - Management Consulted is the leading consulting recruiting blog

P3CON-011.title: The New Requirement for MBAs Seeking Consulting Jobs: AI Proficiency
P3CON-011.author: Bloomberg
P3CON-011.excerpt: MBAs seeking jobs in consulting must be AI proficient. (Article paywalled; title/lede establishes that AI proficiency has become mandatory for MBA consulting hires.)
P3CON-011.relevance: Mainstream business press confirmation that AI proficiency is now an explicit hiring requirement
P3CON-011.credibility: high - Bloomberg primary; abstract only accessed

P3CON-012.title: Management Consulting Report: Pay Is Rising, But New Skills Are Needed
P3CON-012.author: Poets & Quants
P3CON-012.excerpt: BCG and rivals prioritizing AI strategy and value identification, MLOps, applied GenAI and prompt design, change management for AI adoption, AI governance and risk. BCG limiting generalist MBA hiring while prioritizing engineering and AI talent; introducing 'T-shaped consultant' model. BCG: 25% of $14.4B 2025 revenue (~$3.6B) came from AI work; 40% of McKinsey client work has AI component. Consultants with AI expertise earn ~10% more.
P3CON-012.relevance: Concrete revenue and hiring-mix data showing AI is the primary growth and skills vector
P3CON-012.credibility: high - Poets&Quants is the leading MBA/consulting trade press

P3CON-013.title: What AI Really Means for Consulting
P3CON-013.author: Consulting Success
P3CON-013.excerpt: Surface-level analysis and generic frameworks are no longer worth premium fees. The new value prop is deep expertise, original thinking, distinctive frameworks, trusted advisor relationships. The more precisely you own a specific problem, the harder you are to replace. AI handles poorly: original strategic judgment in specific client context, relationship-based work, anything requiring accountability. Invisible integration is the goal.
P3CON-013.relevance: Establishes the new positioning logic for independent consultants in the AI era
P3CON-013.credibility: medium-high - leading practitioner advisory site for independents

P3CON-014.title: The Fourth Signal: AI Visibility for Independent Consultants
P3CON-014.author: Syntari
P3CON-014.excerpt: Identity without evidence is a claim. Credibility requires proof that has been documented, published, and indexed. Independent practitioners who build structured proof systems early (case libraries, framework repositories, published outcome summaries) achieve better results. AI visibility is the degree to which AI search systems (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews) can find, evaluate, and recommend a consultant. Every engagement is potential AI content: quantified outcomes, named case studies, video testimonials, published results.
P3CON-014.relevance: Direct articulation of the 'evidence-based identity' shift that maps to our product thesis
P3CON-014.credibility: medium - vendor blog but argument is well-developed and consistent with broader signals

P3CON-015.title: Keep it Confidential: How to Showcase Your NDA-Protected Design Work
P3CON-015.author: Interaction Design Foundation
P3CON-015.excerpt: NDAs vary enormously. Standard tactics: ask employer for permission, anonymize (remove logos/brand names/distinctive color schemes/identifying data), focus on process over final product, create generic case studies (project scope/role/goals/outcomes with hypothetical visuals), develop supplementary personal projects. Avoid blacking out parts of case studies - high NDA-violation risk and bad reading experience.
P3CON-015.relevance: Confirms the structural problem: consultants legally cannot show real deliverables, must construct sanitized proxies - perfect product opportunity
P3CON-015.credibility: high - IxDF is a respected design-education institution

P3CON-016.title: LinkedIn is my #1 Source of New Clients as a Consultant
P3CON-016.author: Buffer
P3CON-016.excerpt: Around 80% of one consultant's 2024 clients reached out via LinkedIn. LinkedIn is where professional discovery happens at scale; potential clients check LinkedIn before any meeting. Personal website is for depth and brand control - service descriptions, case studies, testimonials, pricing, booking.
P3CON-016.relevance: Confirms LinkedIn dominance for client discovery in consulting - constraint on any alternative identity product
P3CON-016.credibility: medium - single-consultant case study but consistent with cross-source claim that ~73% of fractional execs get clients via LinkedIn

P3CON-017.title: Fractional Executive Personal Brand Guide
P3CON-017.author: GetAFractional
P3CON-017.excerpt: In the fractional economy, your personal brand is your pipeline. 73% of fractional executives secure clients through LinkedIn. 92.8% of fractional professionals acquired clients through referrals and networking. 'Fractional CMO' is generic; 'Fractional CMO for B2B SaaS scaling $2M-$10M ARR' is memorable. Most successful fractional execs have one primary channel (often LinkedIn) and 1-2 supporting.
P3CON-017.relevance: Quantifies channel mix and confirms personal brand IS the product for this segment
P3CON-017.credibility: medium-high - specialized fractional-economy site with consistent data

P3CON-018.title: AI Consultant Cost US 2026: $600-$1,200/day Rates
P3CON-018.author: Nicola Lazzari
P3CON-018.excerpt: Independent consultants charge $600-1,200/day; Big 4 firms command $2,500-3,500+/day. Individual AI consultants typically charge $800-$1,500/day for mid-senior expertise; top independents (ex-FAANG, PhD) command $1,500+. Boutique firms $1,200-$2,000+/day, 50-70% less than enterprise consultancies, 40% faster time-to-value. Specialized GenAI/RL adds 20-30% premium.
P3CON-018.relevance: Pricing data establishing that independent consultants can command premium rates - high LTV potential users
P3CON-018.credibility: medium - pricing analyst blog, numbers triangulate with other sources

P3CON-019.title: Ex-McKinsey consultant confirms in the AI Era, Boutiques are Winning
P3CON-019.author: The Finance Story
P3CON-019.excerpt: Boutique consulting firms growing 2-3x faster than MBB with AI leveling the field. Some clocking 100-200% YoY. Boutiques charge 30-40% less than traditional firms on average. AI-driven boutiques excel by focusing on niche/industry-specific applications. Thousands of niche firms expected to emerge focused on sectors/regions/problems.
P3CON-019.relevance: Confirms structural shift toward small/independent consulting - the segment that needs identity products
P3CON-019.credibility: medium - claim attributed to ex-McKinsey practitioner, consistent with broader narrative

P3CON-020.title: Indie AI Consulting: Positioning, Pricing, and Proposals (Jason Liu)
P3CON-020.author: Jason Liu / Maven
P3CON-020.excerpt: Jason Liu built his AI consulting practice from scratch; consistently closes five-figure solo deals, maintains waitlist of clients. 3-day course on positioning, pricing, proposals. Private Circle community of AI consultants. Quote: 'ai consulting has given me all kinds of freedoms... only once i stopped pricing for my time...'
P3CON-020.relevance: Direct evidence of paid education market for indie AI consultants - signals high motivation to invest in identity/practice tools
P3CON-020.credibility: high - direct course page and creator's X post

P3CON-021.title: Independent Consultants: Build Your Practice | Catalant
P3CON-021.author: Catalant
P3CON-021.excerpt: Catalant: subject-matter experts, former Fortune 500 operators, EMs and Partners from Big 3 consulting firms. AI-powered platform with predictive project scoping and automated team assembly. $150-$300/hour consultants, enterprise focus. Consulting marketplace sector ~$3.2B in 2025, driving 40% of independent consulting deals.
P3CON-021.relevance: Shows ex-MBB independents have multiple existing marketplaces - product competition for identity ownership

P3CON-022.title: Consulting industry in the United States - statistics & facts
P3CON-022.author: Statista / BLS
P3CON-022.excerpt: ~840,000 management consultants in US (2023, BLS); some sources cite ~1M. Global consulting headcount ~3M professionals in 2023. US management consulting industry projected ~$466.7B in 2026. BLS projects 9.4% employment growth from 2024-2034 for management, scientific, and technical consulting services.
P3CON-022.relevance: Hard market sizing - establishes the addressable user pool

P3CON-023.title: McKinsey's Lilli: The AI Revolutionizing Consulting with PowerPoint Precision
P3CON-023.author: OpenTools
P3CON-023.excerpt: Since early 2025 McKinsey consultants turn a short prompt into a client-ready slide deck or proposal in Lilli. One-Click agent stitches approved templates, charts, narrative into polished PowerPoint. Tone of Voice checker rewrites text to match firm's style guide.
P3CON-023.relevance: Specific evidence that AI now produces consultant deliverables end-to-end inside the prestige firm

P3CON-024.title: Deckster AI: BCG presentation engine
P3CON-024.author: Skywork
P3CON-024.excerpt: Deckster, developed by BCG, is a generative AI PowerPoint plugin training on 800-900 templates; helps consultants rapidly create polished decks. 'Review this' feature helps junior consultants optimize slides to senior standards. ~40% of associates use Deckster weekly. Bain integrates Microsoft Copilot across 13,000 consultants.
P3CON-024.relevance: BCG and Bain analog to Lilli; shows the slide-deck (core consultant artifact) workflow is being industrialized by AI
P3CON-024.credibility: medium - tech site aggregating public info

P3CON-025.title: How AI is Redefining Strategy Consulting: McKinsey, BCG, and Bain
P3CON-025.author: Takafumi Endo / Medium
P3CON-025.excerpt: Demand will grow for experienced mid- to senior-level talent, particularly manager+ where talent shortage already exists. BCG hiring fewer MBAs, prioritizing tech and data scientists. 55% of organizations piloting or in production with GenAI but lack talented professionals. AI consultant rates higher than standard MC, supply-demand mismatch.
P3CON-025.relevance: Concentrates the structural shift: junior leverage contracts, mid-senior with AI fluency becomes scarce/expensive

P3CON-026.title: McKinsey & Company launches internal generative AI tool Lilli
P3CON-026.author: Consultancy-me
P3CON-026.excerpt: Description of Lilli rollout, RAG architecture, 100K+ documents, change-management playbook; complementary article confirming primary source claims about Lilli adoption inside McKinsey.
P3CON-026.relevance: Triangulates Lilli claims with industry trade press
